speaker: Lynette
content: Here's what I pulled for Eddie. I should warn you that most of the clothes in there aren't that stylish.
mark: pull for 为……加油，支持……
stylish adj. 时髦的，流行的，有格调的；潇洒的

speaker: Huber
content: don't worry about it. Eddie's a beggar now, which means she can't be a chooser. Of course, we don't have to add salt to the wound.
mark: beggar n. 乞丐，叫花子；
add salt to the wound 在伤口上撒盐 wound n. 创伤，伤口；损害，痛苦

speaker: Lynette
content: Listen, I was just getting dinner ready...
mark: get ready 准备好 get sth ready 把某物准备好

speaker: Huber
content: Say no more. I'll get out of your hair. By the way, was that you I saw getting pulled over by a policeman?
mark: say no more 不必再说了
get out of your hair 不打扰你了，不再烦扰你，让你一个人待着。
by the way 顺便说一下
get pulled over by a policeman 被警察拦下  get pulled over通常用来描述司机被警察拦下的情况。pull over: 靠边停车

speaker: Lynette
content: Yeah, the boys were acting up in the car, I couldn't get them to sit down.
mark: get sb to do sth 让某人做某事；使某人做某事

speaker: Huber
content: Young boys can be so willful.
mark: willful adj. 有意的；任性的；故意的

speaker: Lynette
content: I try everything. I scream, I threaten, I reason, I beg, nothing works.
mark: scream v. 尖叫；高声喊，大声叫；
perverse adj. 任性的，故意作对的；有悖常理的，反常的，不合人意的
joy n. 欢欣，高兴；使人高兴的人（或物），乐趣；成功，满足
get joy out of 固定搭配，意思是“从……中获得乐趣” out of 因为……而做某事
get...out of...  表示从…… 中获得……

speaker: Lynette
content: I don't know if it's because they're too young and they don't understand, or if they're just getting some perverse joy out of testing me.

speaker: Huber
content: My mother used to have the worst time with me in the car, so one time when I was acting up, she stopped and left me on the side of the road, and she drove off.
mark: one time when...有一次当…… 的时候
on the side of the road 在路边 == by the side of the road
drive off 驾车离去

speaker: Huber
content: Oh, she came back immediately, but I never misbehaved in the car again. You should try that.
mark: immediately adv. 立即，马上；紧接地，紧靠地；直接地
misbehave v. 行为不端，举止失礼；发生故障，失灵

speaker: Lynette
content: Mrs. Huber, I could never leave my kids by the side of the road.

speaker: Huber
content: When it comes to discipline, sometimes you must be creative. My mother knew that, smart lady.
mark: discipline n/v. 纪律，风纪；惩罚；训导；训练，锻炼；科目，学科
creative adj. 创造（性）的，创作的；有创造力的，有想象力的
she's in a home now, and her mind has just turned to mush. 她现在住在养老院，她的思想变得一团糟。in a home 通常表示她现在在某个住所（可能是养老院、福利院等类似的机构场所，也可能只是普通的别人家或某个居住的地方）
turn to mush 变得软糊；引申为形容人的头脑等变得混乱糊涂
mush n. 感伤的话；浓粥；软块；胆怯

speaker: Huber
content: Of course, she's in a home now, and her mind has just turned to mush.

speaker: Lynette
content: Well, like I said, I should go back and get dinner ready.
mark: like I said 就像我说的;正如我所说
